explanation: This LTL property ensures that whenever apnea is detected and the system is currently operating in PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation) mode, then in the very next time step, the system will transition to PCV (Pressure Controlled Ventilation) mode, and concurrently, its respiratory rate (RR), inspiratory pressure (Pinsp), and I:E ratio (ItoE) will be set to their pre-determined apnea backup values (RR_AP, PinspAP, and I:E_AP respectively). This directly translates the conditional automatic switch and setting of parameters upon apnea detection while in PSV mode.

The property states that globally (G), if apnea is detected while the system is in Pressure Support Ventilation (PSV) mode (bool_exp1 = apnea & PSVMode), then eventually (F), the system shall switch to Pressure Controlled Ventilation (PCV) mode (PCVMode), stop being in PSV mode (!PSVMode), and have its respiratory rate, inspiratory pressure, and I:E ratio set to their pre-determined apnea backup values (RR_equalTo_RR_AP & P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP & ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP). This captures the requirement for an automatic switch from PSV to PCV mode with specific settings upon apnea detection.

The property states that whenever apnea is detected (bool_exp1) while the system is in PSV mode (bool_exp2), the system must automatically switch to and remain in PCV mode with the specified apnea backup settings (bool_exp3). The ‘G[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3’ or ‘G bool_exp3’ ensures that this state persists for at least N_DURATION time units or indefinitely, reflecting the continuous nature of a backup mode after an automatic switch.

The property states that globally, if apnea is detected while the system is currently operating in Pressure Support Ventilation (PSV) mode, then eventually the system will automatically transition to Pressure Controlled Ventilation (PCV) mode. This transition also entails that the system will no longer be in PSV mode, and all the specified pre-determined apnea backup settings for respiratory rate (RR_AP), inspiratory pressure (PinspAP), and I:E ratio (I:E AP) will be active. The use of 'F' (Finally/Eventually) captures the 'shall automatically switch' aspect, indicating that the new state will be reached at some point in the future after the condition is met.

The property states that globally, if apnea is detected while the system is in PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation) mode, then eventually, the system will switch to PCV (Pressure Controlled Ventilation) mode. Concurrently, the respiratory rate (RR), inspiratory pressure (P_insp), and inspiratory-to-expiratory ratio (I:E) will be set to their respective apnea backup values (RR_AP, P_inspAP, and ItoE_AP). This captures the automatic mode switch and parameter adjustment upon apnea detection.

The property states that globally, if apnea is detected while the system is in PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation) mode, then in the very next state, the system must be in PCV (Pressure Controlled Ventilation) mode, and simultaneously, its respiratory rate (RR), inspiratory pressure (Pinsp), and inspiratory-to-expiratory ratio (I:E) must be set to their respective apnea backup values (RR_AP, P_inspAP, and I:E_AP). This captures the immediate and automatic transition from PSV to PCV mode with the specified backup settings upon apnea detection.

The property states that globally, if apnea is detected while the system is in PSV mode (bool_exp2), then in the very next state, the system shall be in PCV mode and all the specified apnea backup settings (RR_AP, PinspAP, I:E_AP) shall be applied (bool_exp3). The use of the 'X' (Next) operator captures the immediate 'switch' implied by the natural language. N_DURATION is set to null as no specific duration is mentioned in the prompt.

The property states that globally, if apnea is detected while the system is in PSV mode, then in the very next state, the system shall be in PCV mode with the respiratory rate set to the apnea respiratory rate, the inspiratory pressure set to the apnea inspiratory pressure, and the I:E ratio set to the apnea I:E ratio. This precisely captures the automatic mode switch from PSV to PCV and the application of specified backup settings upon apnea detection.

The property states that globally, if apnea is detected while the system is in PSV mode, then in the very next state, the system shall be in PCV mode and the specified apnea backup settings (respiratory rate equal to RR_AP, inspiratory pressure equal to P_inspAP, and I:E ratio equal to I:E_AP) shall be active. This directly captures the conditional switch with immediate effect and the application of all required backup parameters. The 'switch from PSV to PCV' is modeled by ensuring PSV is true on the left-hand side of the implication and PCV (along with the new settings) is true in the next state.

The property states that globally, whenever apnea is detected and the system is currently in PSV mode, then in the very next state, the system shall be in PCV mode, and its respiratory rate (RR) shall be set to the apnea respiratory rate (RR_AP), the inspiratory pressure (P_insp) shall be set to the apnea inspiratory pressure (P_inspAP), and the inspiratory-to-expiratory (I:E) ratio shall be set to the apnea I:E ratio (ItoE_AP). This captures the automatic transition from PSV to PCV mode with specific backup settings upon apnea detection, as described in the prompt.
